name: Goose Two Ways
steps: Brine the bird in 5% salt brine, 1 tbsp ground toasted sichuan pepper for 48hr
Take out of the brine, pat dry
Let it air dry in walk in fridge at least overnight
Deep fry the shallot in goose fat until golden
Before cooking, always double check the skin is properly dried
Set Josper to about 320-350C
Place the goose in a tray where it can collect the fat from
Pop into the josper oven to roast for 2mins, turn and roast for another 2min
Take out, cover with tin foil and rest for 30mins
Take the leg off, and poach
Repeat roasting the crown for another 3 times
The internal temperature should reach about 55C
Always probe to check if it needs more or less cooking
Thinly sliced the goose breasts to 1-2mm slices, about 9-11 slices
Serve with finely julienned ginger on one side
1st cook the whole goose, then take the leg off
Brown the trimmings, carcass and bones
Add the browned bones, ginger and spring onion, Shao Xin wine and the stock into the pot
Simmer the stock for 4 hrs. skim as you go.
Drain and keep the clear broth to use for poaching goose legs
Debone and string tie the goose legs to nice long piece
Poach the goose leg in the goose broth for 1.5hrs, simmering. Check sizes!
Bring the goose broth to boil, add the mustard greens
Gently bring back to simmer. Taste the broth as the mustard greens could be salty. Not to over boil as it makes broth cloudy
Slice the goose legs into 3mm slices. Warm it up gently
Lay it neatly over the top of mustard green, pour broth over
Remove the seeds and chop the fermented chillies very fine
In each ramekin, put in 1tsp Fermented red chilli, 1 tsp spring onion and top with 1tbsp aged soy

notes: Crispy shallot used for lard rice
DO NOT pre mix this sauce. Aged soy is precious.
No aggressive boiling, Skim as you go. No sous vide on duck leg. Broth must be clear.
